title: Delivery Tags, Acknowledgements, and Redelivery
description: A Go 1.26 service team must keep delivery tags scoped to the owning channel generation and choose exactly one Ack, Nack, or Reject after effect evidence; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. Learners cumulatively produce a same-channel terminal delivery decision record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose manual or automatic acknowledgement from loss tolerance, consumer memory, work duration, and effect ownership; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Acknowledge, nack, or reject a delivery exactly once on its owning channel and prevent stale or cross-channel tag use; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Transfer ownership only after the intended durable effect, transactional inbox record, or safely repeatable handoff succeeds; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
